ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for bamboo salt white coffee. FOODON_03301099 (coffee beverage) is the closest defensible parent class. Bamboo salt and non-dairy creamer components are not individually represented.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW for white coffee with milk/creamer is the closest defensible match. Bamboo salt infusion is a product-specific modifier not captured in FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for bamboo salt white coffee or sufficiently close beverage subtype. Omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

allergens: milk (condensed milk, non-dairy creamer may contain milk derivatives)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 250 ml (250 g), equivalent to a regular takeaway cup of cold white coffee as commonly served at Singaporean food establishments.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Beverages, coffee, instant, with whitener and sugar (NDB 14215); cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Coffee, brewed, prepared with tap water (NDB 14209)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for 'Bamboo Salt White Coffee (Cold)' exists in any approved source. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 14215 (Beverages, coffee, instant, with whitener and sugar), which is the closest approved-source analogue for an instant white coffee with non-dairy creamer and sugar. HPB Singapore Foodo does not publish a directly accessible machine-readable entry for bamboo salt white coffee. Bamboo salt contributes a modest sodium increment (~10–15 mg/100 g above standard instant white coffee); the sodium value of 60 mg/100 g is consistent with this adjustment. Input values from GrabFood (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) are retained where they align with the USDA adapted range. All per_100g values are treated as estimated.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Analogous food: Coffee, instant, with sugar and whitener (GI ~53, glucose reference)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No GI value exists in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature specifically for bamboo salt white coffee. The closest analogous food is instant coffee with sugar and non-dairy whitener (GI ~53, glucose scale), as reported in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). GI 53 falls in the low category (GI ≤55 = low per University of Sydney classification). Bamboo salt is a mineral salt additive that does not materially alter the glycemic response of the beverage. GL per serving calculated as: GI (53) × carbohydrate per serving (6.8 g/100 g × 250 g = 17 g) / 100 = 9.01, rounded to 9.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Sweetened instant coffee mix with non-dairy creamer; saturated fat 1.8 g/100 g; low potassium; added sugar load
hypertension.guidance: Although sodium is relatively low at 60 mg/100 g, this sweetened instant white coffee contains non-dairy creamer (likely palm-oil-based, contributing saturated fat) and added sugar with negligible potassium and fibre, which collectively impair vascular health; patients with hypertension should choose unsweetened black coffee (kopi o kosong) instead.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) with added condensed milk and sugar; GI ~53 (low-medium boundary); GL ~9.1 per 250 ml serving; negligible dietary fibre (0.2 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: This beverage qualifies as a sugar-sweetened beverage with 4.8 g sugar/100 g (12 g per 250 ml serving), a glycemic index of approximately 53, and a glycemic load of ~9.1 per serving; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this drink and substitute with unsweetened black coffee or kopi o kosong to prevent postprandial glucose spikes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 1.8 g/100 g from non-dairy creamer; trans fat 0.04 g/100 g; added sugar; ultra-processed formulation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The non-dairy creamer component contributes saturated fat (1.8 g/100 g) and a small amount of trans fat (0.04 g/100 g), both of which raise LDL cholesterol; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this sweetened instant white coffee and prefer plain brewed coffee without added creamer or sugar.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Trans fat and saturated fat from non-dairy creamer; added refined sugar; no anti-inflammatory nutrients
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The non-dairy creamer in this formulation likely contains palm-oil-based or partially hydrogenated fats contributing pro-inflammatory saturated and trans fats; combined with refined sugar from condensed milk and the absence of omega-3, fibre, or antioxidant vitamins, this beverage is unsuitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bamboo Salt White Coffee (Cold) is a chilled instant white coffee beverage infused with bamboo salt (竹盐), served at Chinese hot pot restaurants in Singapore. It is prepared by dissolving instant white coffee powder — a blend of robusta coffee, non-dairy creamer, sugar, and condensed milk — in water, adding a small quantity of bamboo salt for a subtle mineral-saline note, and serving the mixture chilled or over ice. The flavour profile is sweet, creamy, and mildly bitter from the robusta coffee base, with a faint saline undertone from the bamboo salt. The texture is smooth and milky due to the non-dairy creamer. Nutritionally, per 100 ml the beverage provides approximately 48 kcal, 0.6 g protein, 2.0 g fat (of which 1.8 g is saturated), 6.8 g carbohydrate, and 4.8 g sugar, with 60 mg sodium. The saturated fat and trans fat (0.04 g/100 g) originate from the non-dairy creamer, which is typically palm-oil-based. The beverage is classified as ultra-processed and qualifies as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB). It has a glycemic index of approximately 53 (low category, GI ≤55) and a glycemic load of approximately 9.1 per 250 ml serving. Clinically, it is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its added sugar and moderate GI, and it raises cardiovascular and hypertension concerns due to its saturated and trans fat content. It is vegetarian and generally considered halal and gluten-free, but is not dairy-free due to condensed milk and possible milk derivatives in the creamer. Bamboo salt (Korean origin: 죽염, Chinese: 竹盐) is produced by packing sea salt into bamboo tubes and roasting them at high temperatures, yielding a mineral-rich salt used in East and Southeast Asian functional food and beverage products.
common_user_queries: What is bamboo salt white coffee?
Is bamboo salt white coffee healthy?

Is bamboo salt white coffee suitable for diabetics?
Is cold white coffee high in sugar?
Can I drink white coffee if I have hypertension?

What does bamboo salt taste like in coffee?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for bamboo salt white coffee exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore, USDA FoodData Central, IMR Malaysia MyFCD). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 14215 (Beverages, coffee, instant, with whitener and sugar), which is the closest approved-source analogue. The input values (GrabFood, ai_generated, confidence 0.7) are consistent with the adapted range and are retained as estimated values. GI is an analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care, peer-reviewed). Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient profile, and moisture data for this specific bamboo salt instant white coffee formulation are not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 14215 or any other approved source as of 2026-04-17. 2. ZERO VALUES: cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed as negligible/zero for non-dairy creamer-based instant coffee beverages per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 14215 (cholesterol 0 mg/100 g). vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero; coffee and non-dairy creamer contain no meaningful vitamin C. 3. NUTRIENT ADAPTATION: All per_100g values are estimated, adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 14215. Bamboo salt contributes a modest sodium increment above standard instant white coffee; the 60 mg/100 g sodium value is consistent with this. 4. GI CATEGORY CORRECTION (Iteration 3): gi_category corrected from 'medium' to 'low'. Per University of Sydney GI classification, GI ≤55 = low. GI of 53 therefore falls in the low category. Previous iteration incorrectly labelled this as 'medium'. 5. SOURCE COMPLIANCE CORRECTION (Iteration 3): nutrient_source updated to cite USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 14215 as the primary approved source. Previous iteration cited HPB Singapore Foodo as a co-primary source; HPB Foodo does not publish a directly accessible machine-readable entry for this product and its citation was flagged as a forbidden/unsupported source reference. USDA FoodData Central is an approved secondary global source per PROJECT_VERITAS_Ver2.md Section 5.1. 6. PORTIONS: Two portion entries were present in the input (250 g and 300 g both marked is_default: true). The 250 g entry is adopted as the canonical default serving for GL calculation. 7. DUAL is_default FLAG: Input contained two portions both flagged is_default: true — this is a data quality issue in the source record; 250 g is used as the canonical default. 8. ONTOLOGY: FoodOn FOODON_03301099 and FoodEx2 A0EJW are approximate matches; no exact ontology code exists for this product. SNOMED CT omitted to preserve ontology honesty.
